name: Alpera — vuelta por La Dehesa y el Molinar
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Alpera, Spain, traverse a landscape characterized by limestone crags and Mediterranean woodland, offering diverse terrain for riders. The region features green spaces like La Mejorada and Félix Rodriguez de la Fuente Park, providing accessible areas for outdoor activity. While specific mountain biking infrastructure is not widely detailed, the natural environment supports a variety of routes. This area in Castile-La Mancha presents a mix of elevations and natural features suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Alpera The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra del Mugrón and Almansa Reservoir Circular, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 5 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Hunde and Pico Palomera Route, a moderate 27.9 miles (44.9 km) path that climbs through varied terrain towards Pico Palomera. Local mountain bikers also love the Almansa and Santa Bárbara - Sierra de la Oliva Loop, a 31.9 miles (51.4 km) trail leading through the Sierra de la Oliva, often completed in about 4 hours 41 minutes. Mountain biking around Alpera is defined by limestone crags, Mediterranean woodland, and green park spaces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate climbs to longer circular routes. The routes in Alpera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alpera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Alpera?
A: There are over 100 mountain bike trails around Alpera, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored more than 100 routes in the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Alpera?
A: The mountain bike trails around Alpera traverse a diverse landscape characterized by limestone crags and Mediterranean woodland. You'll encounter a mix of elevations, from moderate climbs to longer circular routes, often passing through green spaces like La Mejorada and Félix Rodriguez de la Fuente Park.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Alpera?
A: Yes, Alpera offers several trails suitable for beginners. Out of the over 100 routes, 14 are classified as easy, providing a good starting point for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Alpera?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult mountain bike routes around Alpera. These trails typically feature more significant elevation gains and technical sections, providing a demanding experience.

Q: What natural and historical attractions can I see while mountain biking near Alpera?
A: While mountain biking near Alpera, you can explore significant natural and historical sites. The region is home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Vieja Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its prehistoric rock art. Other natural monuments include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4827318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arco de San Pascual&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4554111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Piojo&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Almansa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, several trails offer scenic viewpoints. For example, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4713383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Museum Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Many routes also traverse areas with natural beauty, such as the Sierra del Mugrón, offering picturesque vistas.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Alpera?
A: Absolutely. Many mountain bike routes around Alpera are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036342633/sierra-del-mugron-and-almansa-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Mugrón and Almansa Reservoir Circular&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036350524/almansa-and-santa-bárbara-sierra-de-la-oliva-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almansa and Santa Bárbara - Sierra de la Oliva Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1328874000/la-hunde-pico-palomera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hunde - Pico Palomera Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alpera?
A: The mountain bike trails in Alpera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 250 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the natural beauty of the limestone crags and Mediterranean woodland, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails around Alpera suitable for families?
A: While specific family-friendly mountain biking infrastructure is not widely detailed, the presence of green spaces like La Mejorada and Félix Rodriguez de la Fuente Park suggests areas suitable for easier rides. For families, consider exploring some of the 14 easy-rated trails, which typically have less challenging terrain and elevation.

Q: Is Alpera a good destination for mountain biking with dogs?
A: Alpera's natural environment, with its Mediterranean woodland and open spaces, can be suitable for mountain biking with dogs. However, always ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry enough water for both of you. Check local regulations for specific trail access with pets.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Alpera?
A: The best time to go mountain biking in Alpera is generally during the spring and autumn months. The weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. This allows for comfortable rides through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the mountain bike trails?
A: Alpera is a municipality with local amenities. While specific cafes directly on trails are not highlighted, you can find cafes and accommodation options within Alpera itself. Planning your route to pass through or near the town will allow you to access these facilities for refreshments or an overnight stay.

Q: How long do mountain bike routes in Alpera typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike routes in Alpera varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036342633/sierra-del-mugron-and-almansa-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Mugrón and Almansa Reservoir Circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 30.6-mile (49.3 km) trail, typically takes about 4 hours 5 minutes. Shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1328874000/la-hunde-pico-palomera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hunde - Pico Palomera Loop&lt;/a&gt; (14.3 miles / 23 km) can be completed in around 2 hours 5 minutes.
tips: Important archaeological remains such as flint tools, animal bone remains and human remains have been discovered. In addition, numerous cave paintings have been found that represent animals, human figures and geometric shapes. It has been the subject of numerous studies by archaeologists and paleontologists since it was discovered in the 19th century.

The Camino de Levante passes through several abandoned villages. Casas de Don Pedro is one of the largest villages in the area. Today it is mostly in ruins. Some corrals have been transformed into agricultural warehouses but no houses are currently inhabited. You will find a stream and a source with drinking water in this village. So it can be a good place to stop to take a breather.

In spring you can see a green mantle created by the vineyards in the area for hectares.

waypoints.name: Cueva de la Vieja Rock Art Site
